notes: Based on Stull chart (1912) as implemented on Glazy.org. Matte: ratio < 4, Semi-matte (satin): ratio ~4-5, Glossy: ratio > 5. High-flux glazes (CaO, B2O3) may be glossy at lower ratios. These thresholds are for typical stoneware glazes. Low-fire glazes follow different rules. The SiO2:Al2O3 ratio is the single most useful predictor of surface character, but it's not the only factor — flux type, firing temperature, and cooling rate all matter.
caveats: High CaO can promote gloss at lower SiO2:Al2O3 ratios
High MgO can promote matte at higher SiO2:Al2O3 ratios
B2O3 increases fluidity, potentially lowering effective matte threshold
Underfired glazes may appear matte regardless of ratio
Cooling rate affects crystal formation and surface development
